item.title: UCB/Neurona Deal
item.description:
• Summary: UCB has agreed to acquire Neurona Therapeutics for $1.15 billion, expanding its neurology portfolio with cell therapies for epilepsy. The deal highlights UCB's strategic push into advanced therapies and Neurona's pipeline in regenerative medicine for neurological disorders.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, Fierce Biotech

item.title: Kailera Therapeutics IPO & Pipeline
item.description:
• Summary: Kailera Therapeutics raised $625 million in a record-breaking IPO to fund its obesity drug pipeline, including candidates licensed from Chinese partners like Hengrui. The company aims to compete in the competitive obesity market with novel therapies, leveraging global development strategies.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech, BioPharma Dive, BioSpace

item.title: Tevas Ajovy vs. Lilly Patent Dispute
item.description:
• Summary: Teva and Eli Lilly are engaged in a patent dispute over Ajovy (fremanezumab), a migraine drug. The Federal Circuit reversed a prior loss for Teva, potentially impacting market exclusivity and competition in the CGRP inhibitor class.
  • MoAs: Ajovy is a monoclonal antibody that targets the calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) receptor, preventing migraine attacks by inhibiting neurogenic inflammation.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Foundayo (Lilly's Digital Platform)
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly launched Foundayo, a digital platform for managing chronic conditions like obesity and diabetes, which reached over 1,390 patients in its first week. This initiative enhances patient engagement and competes with rivals like Novo Nordisk's oral Wegovy.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioSpace

item.title: Astellas/GSK/Daiichi Corporate Updates
item.description:
• Summary: Corporate updates include: Astellas rethinking its stem cell therapy strategy, GSK expressing bullishness on its ADC plans, and Daiichi Sankyo selling its OTC business. These moves reflect portfolio adjustments and strategic focuses in biopharma.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Trevi Therapeutics Funding
item.description:
• Summary: Trevi Therapeutics raised $150 million to establish a biotech fund supporting innovation in Spain and Boston, aiming to accelerate the development of novel therapies.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Lilly Corporate Updates
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly announced positive results from its oral weight loss pill trial, with potential to reduce clinical trial timelines in the UK. Separately, the company selected a leading candidate for its Nectin-4-targeting antibody-drug conjugate, advancing its oncology pipeline. These updates underscore Lilly's strategic focus on metabolic and cancer therapeutics.
  • MoAs: Oral weight loss pill: GLP-1 receptor agonist (e.g., orforglipron); Nectin-4 antibody-drug conjugate: Targets Nectin-4 for oncology.
  • Sources: STAT News, ApexOnco

item.title: Novartis Corporate Updates
item.description:
• Summary: Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan joined the board of AI company Anthropic, while Structure Therapeutics appointed a new COO from Metsera. These moves reflect corporate leadership shifts and cross-industry collaborations in the biotech sector.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Pharma CEO Pay
item.description:
• Summary: An analysis of 2025 CEO compensation in the pharmaceutical industry reveals trends at major companies like Eli Lilly, Pfizer, AbbVie, GSK, and Johnson & Johnson. Pay packages reflect performance metrics and market dynamics, highlighting corporate governance aspects.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive
